title: boehmeria nivea f. nipononivea
intro: Boehmeria nivea f. nipononivea, also known as Japanese mulberry or hemp milkweed, is a plant species within the nettle family (Urticaceae) and is native to East Asia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. Boehmeria nivea f. nipononivea has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and relieving pain. It is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal purposes, and can also be found in some herbal supplements and tinctures.
